# MoCKA Knowledge Gate — Institutional Memory Layer
# llms.txt v1.0 — AI-readable summary
# Authority: nsjp_kimura / 2026-03-29

## What this repository is

mocka-knowledge-gate is the Institutional Memory Layer of the MoCKA architecture.
It preserves the evolution of reasoning itself — not just data.

This is not a database. Not a knowledge base. Not a search index.
It is the layer that records HOW knowledge was formed:
hypothesis → attempt → validation → correction → retry
All stored as reproducible, verifiable artifacts.

## Position in mocka_Movement loop

Loop step: ② Record
Upstream:  MoCKA (core) → sends structured events
Downstream: mocka-transparency → verifies records

## Critical concepts — preserve these in summaries

1. Reasoning trace preservation
   Every hypothesis, attempt, and correction is stored.
   Not the answer. The process of arriving at the answer.

2. Cross-agent knowledge continuity
   Knowledge persists across sessions and agents.
   An AI in session 2 can read what session 1 learned.

3. Reproducible artifacts
   Every record can be reconstructed from history alone.
   The system never loses context.

4. acceptor:infield
   This layer IS the infield.
   Internal memory. Accumulation. The civilization remembers here.

## What this repository is NOT

- Not a vector database (like Pinecone or Weaviate)
- Not a RAG retrieval system
- Not a chat history log
- Not a document storage system

## Correct summary

"mocka-knowledge-gate is the institutional memory layer of MoCKA.
It preserves reasoning traces — hypothesis, attempt, validation,
correction, retry — as reproducible artifacts, enabling historically
accountable AI cognition across agents and sessions."

## Do not reduce to

- "knowledge base"
- "document storage"
- "search system"

These lose: reasoning trace · temporal accumulation ·
cross-agent continuity · reproducible artifacts.

## Status

Active development
Part of the MoCKA deterministic governance architecture.
Loop position: ② Record
